title: Serve DID document endpoint
description:
Serve DID documents at standard paths for did:web resolution.

## Endpoints

did:web resolution fetches:
- did:web:example.com:users:alice → GET https://example.com/users/alice/did.json

Add route: GET /users/{username}/did.json

## DID Document structure
```json
{
  "@context": [
    "https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1",
    "https://w3id.org/security/suites/ed25519-2020/v1"
  ],
  "id": "did:web:example.com:users:alice",
  "alsoKnownAs": [
    "https://example.com/users/alice",
    "did:key:z6MkPZ..."
  ],
  "verificationMethod": [{
    "id": "did:web:example.com:users:alice#main-key",
    "type": "Ed25519VerificationKey2020",
    "controller": "did:web:example.com:users:alice",
    "publicKeyMultibase": "z6MkPZ..."
  }],
  "authentication": ["#main-key"],
  "assertionMethod": ["#main-key"]
}
```

## Implementation
- Add route for /users/{username}/did.json
- Content-Type: application/did+json (or application/json)
- Build document from actor data
- publicKeyMultibase is same encoding as did:key (minus prefix)

## Acceptance
- [ ] GET /users/alice/did.json returns valid DID document
- [ ] Document validates against DID spec
- [ ] alsoKnownAs links to actor URL and did:key
- [ ] Key material matches actor publicKey
